html:
Exclusive to:

GenericDisconnectedBurningBurnt

Winona's Spotlight is a character-exclusive Structure craftable only by Winona in Don't Starve Together. Requires 1 Trusty Tape, 2 Gold Nuggets, and 1 Firefly to craft.

In order to work, it has to be in range of a powered Winona's Generator or a Winona's G.E.M.erator.

When in a six-tile radius (shown by the circle around it when placing it), the Spotlight will follow the movements of any awake Survivor, shining a bright light centered on the Survivor. If there are multiple Spotlights, they will prioritize having different targets from each other. Spotlights work only at night or while inside a dark place such as the Caves.

Spotlights brightly illuminate a 1.5-Tile radius around the player, a bit brighter than a Torch, going up to a 2.5-Tile radius with the "Spacious Spotlight" skill, similar to a Lantern. Unlike most other light sources, the Spotlight only provides 0.1 tiles of dim light beyond this radius. This may be an issue for players hoping to spot enemies, navigate, or interact with distant objects, as the area will still be completely dark.


Portability

Winona with the "Portability" Insight skill can move her Spotlights by dismantling them and putting them into her inventory. Also, when crafting, it gives Winona's Spotlight as item.

Hotlight

If a Spotlight was placed by Winona with "Hotlight" skill, the light of Spotlights will also radiate 88 units of heat to all players and Thermal Stones during all seasons except summer.

Spacious Spotlight

With the "Spacious Spotlight" skill, the light radius from Spotlight will illuminate a larger area, from a 1.5-Tile radius to a 2.5 Tile radius.

Energy-Saver

The "Energy-Saver" skill will prevent Spotlights from using energy from generators while idle.

Tips

  • Winona's Spotlight gives an effective solution to surviving darkness in the player's base. As it will follow the player's movements, it is excellent for activities that would usually be harder to do at night, especially those that require a free hand such as Farming or cutting down Trees.
    • Winona's Spotlight with the Hotlight upgrade makes this doubly true, as the player does not need to hide near a Fire Pit all night to stay warm while working in the base. However, as Winona's Spotlight only functions during nighttime and not during day or dusk, this is not reliable round the clock, especially during early Spring. This drawback is irrelevant in the Caves, where the Spotlight will be active no matter the time of day on the surface, or during Winter, when Spotlights with the Hotlight upgrade will continue to function even during daytime.
  • Winona's Spotlight with the Hotlight upgrade will warm any player in the radius of the light, even if the Spotlight isn't following the player in question. If there are fewer Spotlights than survivors, the player the Spotlight is following can simply stay in range of other players until daybreak to ensure they stay warm or well-lit.
  • If a Spotlight on land is following a player that hops on a boat and another player on land is in range, the Spotlight will deselect that player as a target and switch which player it's following. This can be used to switch the Spotlight's target without needing to outrange it first.
  • A Spotlight following a target just out of range will illuminate the nearest spot to its target that is within range, rather than turning off immediately.

Trivia

  • Winona's Spotlight, alongside her other gadgets and the Engineering Tab, was introduced in her Character Update as part of the character overhaul for Don't Starve Together.
  • During Rhymes with Play #228, some concepts for Winona's Spotlight were shown, including designs based on a lighthouse, rustic streetlamp, and a studio light.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

Woven - Elegant
Splendid Spotlight
This snazzy spotlight deserves its own spotlight.
Woven - Elegant
Spiked Spotlight
Illuminating while intimidating!

GenericDisconnectedBurningBurnt
“What an ingenious idea!”
–Wilson
“So no more night fires then?”
–Willow
“Fixing lady knows Wolfgang not like dark.”
–Wolfgang
“But what if I liked the dark?”
–Wendy
“THE OVERALLS FLESHLING HAS PROVED USEFUL”
–WX-78
“A sensible safety precaution.”
–Wickerbottom
“Fine, as long as it don't start no forest fires.”
–Woodie
“Well sure, if you want to take the easy way out.”
–Maxwell
“It can't get enöugh öf me!”
–Wigfrid
“She made us a nightlight!”
–Webber
“Oh good. I don't much like the dark out here.”
–Warly
“Friend made Followy Light!”
–Wormwood
“Sorry, Charlie.”
–Winona
“What a funny thing!”
–Wortox
“What this thing?”
–Wurt
“You never know what's out there, lurking in the dark.”
–Walter
“That young lady is a genius!”
–Wanda
“It needs some electricity.”
–Wilson, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Is this thing on?”
–Willow, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Is not working.”
–Wolfgang, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“It's dead...”
–Wendy, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“IT'S JUST SLEEPING”
–WX-78, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“It requires a power source.”
–Wickerbottom, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Does it look like it's working, Lucy?”
–Woodie, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“It's useless out here without power.”
–Maxwell, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“It has nö fighting spirit.”
–Wigfrid, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“How come it's not on?”
–Webber, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Is it out of power?”
–Warly, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Where's light?”
–Wormwood, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Gotta hook it up to the generator.”
–Winona, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Doesn't look too lively!”
–Wortox, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Think it tired.”
–Wurt, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Is it out of power?”
–Walter, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“Maybe it needs winding?”
–Wanda, examining a Spotlight without a Generator.
“It's on fire!”
–Wilson, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Hehehe!”
–Willow, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Is burning!”
–Wolfgang, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Oh no...”
–Wendy, examining a burning Spotlight.
“HURRY, SAVE IT”
–WX-78, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Oh dear, who did this?”
–Wickerbottom, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Woah! Careful, Lucy!”
–Woodie, examining a burning Spotlight.
“I'm going to pretend I don't see it.”
–Maxwell, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Tis engulfed in flame!”
–Wigfrid, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Oh, oh!”
–Webber, examining a burning Spotlight.
“It, oh, it appears to be on fire.”
–Warly, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Help! Help!”
–Wormwood, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Quick! Put it out!”
–Winona, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Hoohoohoo!”
–Wortox, examining a burning Spotlight.
“That not right kind of light.”
–Wurt, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Ah!! That's how forest fires start!”
–Walter, examining a burning Spotlight.
“That doesn't bode well for us.”
–Wanda, examining a burning Spotlight.
“Science couldn't save it.”
–Wilson, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Well, that was mildly entertaining.”
–Willow, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Is all burned up.”
–Wolfgang, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“It all crumbles to ash, in the end.”
–Wendy, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“IT WAS A GOOD MACHINE”
–WX-78, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“I do hope she'll build another.”
–Wickerbottom, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“That's a right shame.”
–Woodie, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Oh. What a pity.”
–Maxwell, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“'Twas devöured by Hel's fire!”
–Wigfrid, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Oh no! It got burnt!”
–Webber, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Crisp, non?”
–Warly, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Oh. So sad”
–Wormwood, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Criminy! My machine!”
–Winona, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“How hilarious!”
–Wortox, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Not gonna help now, florp.”
–Wurt, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“Well... I guess Winona'll have to build a new one.”
–Walter, examining a burnt Spotlight.
“I wonder how quickly she can build a new one...”
–Wanda, examining a burnt Spotlight.
Winona's Spotlight is a character-exclusive Structure craftable only by Winona in Don't Starve Together. Requires 1 Trusty Tape, 2 Gold Nuggets, and 1 Firefly to craft.
In order to work, it has to be in range of a powered Winona's Generator or a Winona's G.E.M.erator.
When in a six-tile radius (shown by the circle around it when placing it), the Spotlight will follow the movements of any awake Survivor, shining a bright light centered on the Survivor. If there are multiple Spotlights, they will prioritize having different targets from each other. Spotlights work only at night or while inside a dark place such as the Caves.
Spotlights brightly illuminate a 1.5-Tile radius around the player, a bit brighter than a Torch, going up to a 2.5-Tile radius with the "Spacious Spotlight" skill, similar to a Lantern. Unlike most other light sources, the Spotlight only provides 0.1 tiles of dim light beyond this radius. This may be an issue for players hoping to spot enemies, navigate, or interact with distant objects, as the area will still be completely dark.
Portability[edit | edit source]
Winona with the "Portability" Insight skill can move her Spotlights by dismantling them and putting them into her inventory.
Also, when crafting, it gives Winona's Spotlight as item.
Hotlight[edit | edit source]
If a Spotlight was placed by Winona with "Hotlight" skill, the light of Spotlights will also radiate 88 units of heat to all players and Thermal Stones during all seasons except summer.
Spacious Spotlight[edit | edit source]
With the "Spacious Spotlight" skill, the light radius from Spotlight will illuminate a larger area, from a 1.5-Tile radius to a 2.5 Tile radius.
Energy-Saver[edit | edit source]
The "Energy-Saver" skill will prevent Spotlights from using energy from generators while idle.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Winona's Spotlight gives an effective solution to surviving darkness in the player's base. As it will follow the player's movements, it is excellent for activities that would usually be harder to do at night, especially those that require a free hand such as Farming or cutting down Trees.
Winona's Spotlight with the Hotlight upgrade makes this doubly true, as the player does not need to hide near a Fire Pit all night to stay warm while working in the base. However, as Winona's Spotlight only functions during nighttime and not during day or dusk, this is not reliable round the clock, especially during early Spring. This drawback is irrelevant in the Caves, where the Spotlight will be active no matter the time of day on the surface, or during Winter, when Spotlights with the Hotlight upgrade will continue to function even during daytime.
Winona's Spotlight with the Hotlight upgrade will warm any player in the radius of the light, even if the Spotlight isn't following the player in question. If there are fewer Spotlights than survivors, the player the Spotlight is following can simply stay in range of other players until daybreak to ensure they stay warm or well-lit.
If a Spotlight on land is following a player that hops on a boat and another player on land is in range, the Spotlight will deselect that player as a target and switch which player it's following. This can be used to switch the Spotlight's target without needing to outrange it first.
A Spotlight following a target just out of range will illuminate the nearest spot to its target that is within range, rather than turning off immediately.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Winona's Spotlight, alongside her other gadgets and the Engineering Tab, was introduced in her Character Update as part of the character overhaul for Don't Starve Together.
During Rhymes with Play #228, some concepts for Winona's Spotlight were shown, including designs based on a lighthouse, rustic streetlamp, and a studio light.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Winona's Spotlight là một Cấu trúc độc quyền dành cho nhân vật chỉ có Winona mới có thể tạo ra trong Don't Starve Together. Cần 1 Băng Trusty, 2 Vàng Thỏi và 1 Đom Đóm để chế tạo.
